name: anthropic
context_prose: E26 anthropic is the shared client for the Anthropic Messages API. It owns the HTTP request shape, the API-version / beta-header pinning, the error sentinels, and the small `CallerFunc` adapter consumed by `recall.NewSummarizer`. Its single external dependency is the Anthropic API (E5), reached via R14 (HTTPS POST /v1/messages). The `cli` component (E21) constructs a `*Client` at startup, wiring in the `HTTPDoer` (`http.DefaultClient`), the resolved API token, and (in tests) an alternate API URL — that wiring is the per-dep decomposition of D2 (cli → anthropic, DI back-edge) shown below in the Dependency Manifest. Recall (E22) consumes the resulting `CallerFunc` purely as a function value; it does not see the `Client` struct.

dependency_manifest.concrete_adapter: value resolved by `tokenresolver.Resolve` (env `ENGRAM_API_TOKEN` or macOS Keychain)

properties.name: Empty token short-circuit
properties.statement: For all calls to `Client.Call` where the configured `token` is the empty string, the method returns `ErrNoToken` and performs no HTTP request.
properties.notes: Guard runs before `doRequest`, so the injected `HTTPDoer` is never invoked.

properties.name: Bearer authorization header
properties.statement: For all successful HTTP requests issued by `Client.Call`, the `Authorization` header equals `"Bearer " + token`.

properties.name: Pinned API version header
properties.statement: For all HTTP requests issued by `Client.Call`, the `Anthropic-Version` header equals `"2023-06-01"`.

properties.name: Pinned beta header
properties.statement: For all HTTP requests issued by `Client.Call`, the `Anthropic-Beta` header equals `"oauth-2025-04-20"`.

properties.name: JSON content-type
properties.statement: For all HTTP requests issued by `Client.Call`, the `Content-Type` header equals `"application/json"` and the body is a JSON-marshalled `request` struct with snake_case fields (`model`, `max_tokens`, `system`, `messages`).
properties.notes: `tagliatelle` lint suppression documents that snake_case is required by the Anthropic API.

properties.name: Context propagation
properties.statement: For all calls `Client.Call(ctx, ...)`, the outgoing HTTP request is constructed with `http.NewRequestWithContext(ctx, …)` so the caller's context governs cancellation and deadlines.
properties.notes: Architectural: required by project rule (`http.NewRequestWithContext` not `http.Get`). No test currently asserts cancellation semantics.

properties.name: Nil response sentinel
properties.statement: For all calls where the injected `HTTPDoer.Do` returns `(nil, nil)`, `Client.Call` returns `ErrNilResponse`.
properties.notes: Guards against doer implementations that violate the `http.RoundTripper` contract.

properties.name: Non-2xx wraps ErrAPIError
properties.statement: For all HTTP responses with status code outside [200, 300), `Client.Call` returns an error that wraps `ErrAPIError` and includes the status code and the API error message (or raw body if not parseable).
properties.notes: Tested with 401 (JSON error body), 502 (non-JSON body), and 429 (rate-limit JSON).

properties.name: Empty content sentinel
properties.statement: For all 2xx responses whose JSON body decodes to a `response` with zero content blocks, `Client.Call` returns `ErrNoContentBlocks`.

properties.name: Returns first content block text
properties.statement: For all 2xx responses with at least one content block, `Client.Call` returns the `text` field of the first content block and a nil error.
properties.notes: Subsequent blocks (if any) are silently dropped — current Haiku usage returns a single text block.

properties.name: Response body always closed
properties.statement: For all paths that reach a non-nil `*http.Response`, `Client.Call` closes `resp.Body` exactly once before returning.
properties.notes: Architectural: deferred close immediately after nil-check. No test directly observes leak.

properties.name: API URL override
properties.statement: For all clients on which `SetAPIURL(url)` has been called, every subsequent `Client.Call` POSTs to `url` instead of the default `https://api.anthropic.com/v1/messages`.
properties.notes: Test-only seam. Production code never calls `SetAPIURL`.

properties.name: Caller delegates to Call
properties.statement: For all `(ctx, model, system, user)` and all `maxTokens`, `c.Caller(maxTokens)(ctx, model, system, user)` returns the same `(string, error)` as `c.Call(ctx, model, system, user, maxTokens)`.
properties.notes: `CallerFunc` is the DI surface consumed by `recall.NewSummarizer`; the closure binds `maxTokens` at construction.

properties.name: Wrapped errors carry package prefix
properties.statement: For all errors returned by `Client.Call` other than the bare sentinels, the error string is prefixed with `"anthropic: "` and wraps the underlying cause via `%w`.
properties.notes: Architectural: project rule requires wrapped errors with context. Sentinel-bearing errors covered by P1, P7-P9.

properties.name: StripCodeFences is total
properties.statement: For all input strings `s`, `StripCodeFences(s)` returns a string without panicking and without returning an error.
properties.notes: Covers fenced ```json, plain ```, preamble+suffix, unclosed fence, no-fence object/array extraction, plain text passthrough, whitespace, empty string.

properties.name: StripCodeFences extracts JSON payload
properties.statement:
For all inputs of the form `prefix + "```json
" + body + "
```" + suffix` (or `prefix + "```
" + body + "
```" + suffix`), `StripCodeFences` returns `body` with surrounding whitespace trimmed; for fenceless inputs containing balanced `{...}` or `[...]`, it returns the outermost balanced substring.
properties.notes: Balanced extraction uses first opener / last matching closer; unbalanced or mismatched delimiters fall through to trimmed input.

properties.name: No direct I/O outside injected doer
properties.statement: For all package-level functions and methods, the only I/O performed is via the injected `HTTPDoer.Do`; no calls to `os.*`, `net/http.Get/Post`, `sql.Open`, or other stdlib I/O escape the package.
properties.notes: Architectural invariant from project DI rule (`internal/` performs I/O only through injected interfaces). Verified by inspection of imports: only `bytes`, `context`, `encoding/json`, `errors`, `fmt`, `io`, `net/http`, `strings`.

properties.name: Model constants pinned
properties.statement: For all consumers of this package, the exported model identifier constants `HaikuModel` and `SonnetModel` resolve to the dated Anthropic model strings (`claude-haiku-4-5-20251001`, `claude-sonnet-4-20250514`) and are the only sanctioned model names within the binary.
properties.notes: Pinning is structural — bumping a model is a one-line edit here that reaches every caller. Recall + learn use `HaikuModel` exclusively today.
